title: Paul Thomas Anderson Finally Wins Oscar After Decades of Snubs
text: Paul Thomas Anderson ended his historic Oscar losing streak at the 98th Academy Awards, winning three statuettes: Best Director, Best Picture, and Best Producer for his film One Battle After Another. Anderson's victory marked a remarkable turnaround for a director who had faced repeated Academy rejections. Over his previous six nominations, acclaimed films like Boogie Nights, There Will Be Blood, and Phantom Thread lost to other contenders. Upon receiving his Best Director award, Anderson remarked, You make a guy work hard for one of these, reflecting both his emotion and disbelief at finally achieving this recognition. One Battle After Another swept through major precursor ceremonies, winning at the BAFTAs, Golden Globes, and Producers Guild Awards. The film starred Leonardo DiCaprio in what many considered a career-best performance and featured strong supporting turns from Teyana Taylor, Benicio del Toro, and Sean Penn. It also became Anderson's highest-grossing film and garnered widespread critical acclaim. The Best Picture race proved extraordinarily competitive against Ryan Coogler's vampire blockbuster Sinners. A late upset at the SAG Awards briefly suggested an historic upset might occur, but ultimately the frontrunner prevailed, delivering Anderson the career validation he had long awaited.
